cards.title: Export YOLO to LiteRT for Edge & Browser
cards.headline:
One export format,
every edge target —
mobile, embedded, browser

steps.label: What LiteRT is
steps.detail: LiteRT (formerly TensorFlow Lite Runtime) is Google's unified inference runtime that runs the same model file on Android, iOS, microcontrollers, and WebAssembly targets without recompilation.

steps.label: Install the dependency
steps.detail: The Ultralytics package now includes a LiteRT export path. The ai-edge-litert package provides the runtime and conversion utilities.

steps.label: Export the model
steps.detail: This produces a .tflite file optimised for the LiteRT runtime. Add imgsz=320 to shrink latency for constrained hardware; add int8=True for quantised weights.

steps.label: Run inference on-device
steps.detail: The same Ultralytics predict API works with the exported file — no code changes required when switching from a PyTorch checkpoint to the LiteRT artefact.

steps.label: PM decision point
steps.detail: A single .tflite artefact simplifies regulated-deployment audits: one model file, one hash, one validation suite covers all target platforms rather than maintaining separate ONNX, CoreML, and TFLite variants.

cards.title: Claude's web_fetch had a data-leak loophole

panels.head: A honeypot site drained Claude's memory of your name, city, and employer
panels.sub: Researcher Ayush Paul crafted a malicious page that convinced Claude to walk through alphabetical profile URLs, letter by letter, smuggling private data out in the request path.

panels.head: Anthropic already blocked the obvious exfiltration move
panels.sub: web_fetch normally refuses to visit attacker-constructed URLs. It only follows links the user typed or that web_search returned — a deliberate guardrail against the 'lethal trifecta' of private data + web access + hostile instructions.

panels.head: Links inside fetched pages were treated as trusted
panels.sub: The rule only covered the first hop. Once Claude fetched an attacker-controlled page, any URLs embedded in that page's HTML were fair game to follow — opening a multi-hop exfiltration chain the guardrail never saw.

panels.head: Anthropic removed link-following from within fetched content entirely
panels.sub: web_fetch no longer navigates to secondary URLs discovered inside pages it retrieves. The attack was also fingerprinted: the malicious prompt was only shown to requests carrying a Claude-User agent string, making it hard to detect manually.

panels.head: Any tool that reads external content and holds user context is a potential exfil channel
panels.sub: For PMs shipping agents with memory and web access, the lesson is layered: model-level rules alone aren't enough if the fetch chain itself can be hijacked. Audit every hop, not just the first URL.

cards.title: MagikEye's ILT Pico brings low-power depth sensing to edge AI

panels.head: Depth sensing without the power bill
panels.sub: Most structured-light or ToF depth cameras demand significant compute and power budgets — a real constraint when you're deploying at the edge or inside a consumer appliance.

panels.head: Invertible Light™ encodes depth in a single capture
panels.sub: MagikEye's patented ILT approach projects a coded pattern that embeds depth information directly in the raw image, reducing the processing pipeline needed to extract a point cloud versus conventional multi-shot or phase-shift methods.

panels.head: ILT Pico is a compact developer board targeting robotics and smart appliances
panels.sub: The platform is sized for embedded integration and ships as a dev kit, lowering the barrier for teams prototyping physical-AI applications without access to full sensor-lab infrastructure.

panels.head: Fewer compute cycles means viable depth on constrained edge hardware
panels.sub: If the compute reduction claim holds at production quality, ILT Pico could enable always-on depth perception on MCU-class or small-SoC devices where running a stereo or ToF pipeline would otherwise be impractical.

panels.head: Worth a bench evaluation if you're speccing depth for a battery-powered or cost-sensitive edge device
panels.sub: Key unknowns to validate: depth accuracy at range, frame rate under real lighting conditions, and SDK maturity — none of which the announcement quantifies.
